subject: Tiếng Anh
instruction: Questions 46-52: Read the passage carefully. Choose an option (A, B, C, or D) that best answers each question.
passage_content:
Feeding the global population as it heads towards 10 billion while also protecting the environment is one of the great challenges of our time. The world’s food sector is a huge contributor to global heating, emitting about 30% of the greenhouse gases created by human activities. But at the same time there is a crisis in food availability as undernutrition and dietary deficiencies coexist with obesity and excessive consumption.
Creating a rational food system that minimises environmental damage while providing a healthy diet for all requires a coordinated approach, says Prof Aedín Cassidy, an expert in nutrition and preventive medicine who is co-director of the Co-Centre for Sustainable Food Systems and director of inter-disciplinary research at the Institute for Global Food Security, at Queen’s University Belfast. “Food production, what we eat, and the environment are all very closely interconnected, and they are key for optimising human health. So how do we transition to a healthy diet from sustainable food systems?” she asks.
Cassidy’s nutritional research has looked at the impact of diet on health conditions such as diabetes and dementia. A 2024 study led by Cassidy shows that consuming food and drink rich in natural compounds known as flavonoids significantly reduces the risks of dementia. A separate study she supervised found that a plant-based diet high in fruit, vegetables and wholegrains greatly reduces the risk of developing type 2 diabetes compared with a plant-based diet high in refined grains, snack foods and desserts.
Sharon Huws, who is a professor in animal science and microbiology, has led several significant studies. One of the latest involves research into reducing methane emissions from sheep, beef and dairy animals by adding locally sourced seaweed to cattle feed. The greatest impact is reducing emissions by up to 80%. “It is the most substantial methane reduction that we’ve ever seen with any dietary intervention we’ve tried,” she says. 150 studies have shown that no trace of the chemical additive is found in any milk produced by cows who have been fed it, so it poses no risk to humans.
As part of its work analysing issues concerned with food production and distribution, the Institute of Global Food Security has conducted extensive research into the role of soil health in sustainable agriculture. Not only does healthy soil act as a carbon sink locking in greenhouse gases, but it also promotes biodiversity. Regenerative farming, which prioritises soil health through promoting biodiversity and reducing tillage and disturbance of the soil, will be vital in addressing the many challenges of sustainable food production.
(Source: Adapted from https://www.theguardian.com/research-to-reality/2025/jan/24/feed-the-world-but-can-we-do-it-without-wrecking-the-planet-further)

questions.question: In paragraph 2, the word “rational” is closest in meaning to?
options: logically designed to balance multiple practical concerns
emotionally appealing and widely accepted by society
traditional and resistant to scientific change
flexible enough to change according to public opinion
questions.explain: Trong ngữ cảnh này, 'rational food system' (hệ thống thực phẩm hợp lý) được định nghĩa tiếp nối là hệ thống vừa 'giảm thiểu thiệt hại môi trường' vừa 'cung cấp chế độ ăn lành mạnh'. Nó tương đương với đáp án A: được thiết kế một cách logic để cân bằng nhiều mối bận tâm thực tiễn.

questions.question: According to paragraph 5, what does the word “it” refer to?
options: carbon sink
healthy soil
agriculture
greenhouse gases
questions.explain: Câu trong bài: 'Not only does healthy soil act as a carbon sink..., but it also promotes biodiversity.' Đại từ 'it' ở vế sau thay thế cho chủ ngữ 'healthy soil' (đất khỏe) ở vế trước.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a challenge facing the global food system?
options: The coexistence of undernutrition and excessive food consumption
The contribution of food production to greenhouse gas emissions
The unequal global distribution of agricultural technology
The environmental impact of current food production practices
questions.explain: Đoạn 1 có nhắc đến nạn suy dinh dưỡng song hành với tiêu thụ quá mức (A), khí nhà kính từ sản xuất thực phẩm (B), và tác động môi trường nói chung (D). Việc phân bố công nghệ nông nghiệp không đồng đều (C) không được đề cập trong bài.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Developing sustainable food systems is essential for human health and environmental protection
Scientific research has proven plant-based diets superior to animal-based diets
Climate change is primarily caused by inefficient global agricultural practices
Soil health is the most critical factor in ensuring future food security
questions.explain: Xuyên suốt bài đọc, tác giả bàn về cách hệ thống thực phẩm ảnh hưởng tới sức khỏe (dinh dưỡng, bệnh tật) và môi trường (khí nhà kính, sức khỏe của đất), từ đó khẳng định việc phát triển hệ thống lương thực bền vững là cần thiết cho cả sức khỏe con người và bảo vệ môi trường.

questions.question: What can be inferred from the studies led or supervised by Prof Cassidy?
options: Not all plant-based diets offer the same health benefits
Dementia is caused primarily by poor dietary choices
Refined grains are more harmful than animal-based foods
Flavonoids completely prevent the development of dementia.
questions.explain: Đoạn 3 đề cập: 'a plant-based diet high in fruit... greatly reduces the risk... compared with a plant-based diet high in refined grains...'. Điều này chứng tỏ hai chế độ ăn đều nguồn gốc thực vật nhưng lại có lợi ích sức khỏe khác nhau, suy ra không phải chế độ ăn thuần chay nào cũng mang lại lợi ích y hệt nhau.

questions.question: The author most likely believes that future food security depends on:
options: Educating consumers without changing production systems.
Increasing global food output regardless of environmental costs
Relying primarily on technological fixes within animal agriculture
A comprehensive transformation of how food is produced and consumed
questions.explain: Tác giả trích lời Prof Cassidy: 'Food production, what we eat, and the environment are all very closely interconnected...' và nêu ra các thay đổi cả về chế độ ăn uống lẫn cách canh tác (regenerative farming, giảm methane). Điều này đòi hỏi sự chuyển đổi toàn diện về cả sản xuất lẫn tiêu thụ.

questions.question: The word “regenerative” in the context of farming most strongly suggests:
options: A return to traditional farming without modern science
A method that prioritises short-term agricultural yields
An approach that restores and enhances ecological systems
A farming model focused exclusively on economic efficiency.
questions.explain: Ngữ cảnh miêu tả 'Regenerative farming' là việc 'ưu tiên sức khỏe của đất thông qua thúc đẩy đa dạng sinh học và giảm thiểu việc cày xới/xáo trộn đất'. Nghĩa là nó hướng tới phương pháp tiếp cận giúp phục hồi và tăng cường các hệ thống sinh thái (restores and enhances ecological systems).

subject: Tiếng Anh
instruction: Questions 53-60: Read the passage carefully. Choose an option (A, B, C, or D) that best answers each question.
passage_content:
Over the past three years, generative artificial intelligence (AI) has had a profound impact on society. AI’s impact on human writing, in particular, has been enormous. The large language models that power AI tools such as ChatGPT are trained on a wide variety of textual data, and they can now produce complex and high-quality texts of their own. Most importantly, the widespread use of AI tools has resulted in hyperproduction of so-called “AI slop”: low-quality AI-generated outputs produced with minimal or even no human effort. Much has been said about what AI writing means for education, work, and culture. But what about science? Does AI improve academic writing, or does it merely produce “scientific AI slop”?
The researchers examined whether use of AI is linked to higher academic productivity, manuscript quality and use of more diverse literature. The number of preprints an author produced was a measure of their productivity, while eventual publication in a journal was a measure of an article’s quality. The study found that when an author started using AI, the number of preprints they produced increased dramatically. Depending on the preprint platform, the overall number of articles an author published per month after adopting AI increased between 36.2% and 59.8%. The increase was biggest among non-native English speakers, and especially for Asian authors, where it ranged from 43% to 89.3%. For authors from English-speaking institutions and with “Caucasian” names, the increase was more modest, in the range of 23.7% to 46.2%. These results suggest AI was often used by non-native speakers to improve their written English.
The study found articles written with AI used more complex language on average than those written without AI. However, among articles written without AI, ones that used more complex language were more likely to be published. This suggests that more complex and high-quality writing is perceived as having greater scientific merit. However, when it comes to articles written with AI support, this relationship was reversed – the more complex the language, the less likely the article was to be published. This suggests that AI-generated complex language was used to hide the low quality of the scholarly work.
AI increased the variety of academic sources. The study also looked at the differences in article downloads originating from Google and Microsoft search platforms. Microsoft’s Bing search engine introduced an AI-powered Bing Chat feature in February 2023. This allowed the researchers to compare what kind of articles were recommended by AI-enhanced search versus regular search engine. Interestingly, Bing users were exposed to a greater variety of sources than Google users, and also to more recent publications. This is likely caused by a technique used by Bing Chat called retrieval-augmented generation, which combines search results with AI prompting.
(Source: Adapted from https://theconversation.com/what-the-hyperproduction-of-ai-slop-is-doing-to-science-272250)

questions.question: In paragraph 1, the word “enormous” can be replaced by ____.
options: ordinary
tiny
miniscule
colossal
questions.explain: Từ 'enormous' có nghĩa là to lớn, khổng lồ. Trong các đáp án, từ đồng nghĩa và có thể thay thế là 'colossal'. Các từ còn lại mang nghĩa bình thường (ordinary) hoặc rất nhỏ bé (tiny, miniscule).

questions.question: The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to ______.
options: preprints
authors
researchers
AI
questions.explain: Trong câu: 'The number of preprints an author produced was a measure of their productivity...', từ 'their' được dùng để chỉ năng suất của các tác giả (an author - sử dụng 'their' mang ý nghĩa số ít trung lập giới tính để chỉ chung về người viết/tác giả).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT discussed in paragraph 2?
options: Differences in publication output before and after AI adoption
Variation in AI benefits between native and non-native English speakers
The percentage increase in productivity among Asian authors
Ethical concerns surrounding authorship and AI usage
questions.explain: Đoạn 2 có nói về sản lượng xuất bản trước và sau AI (A), sự chênh lệch lợi ích giữa người bản xứ và không bản xứ (B), cùng mức tăng phần trăm của các tác giả châu Á (C). Tuy nhiên, các lo ngại về đạo đức (Ethical concerns) khi dùng AI không được đề cập trong đoạn này.

questions.question: Which of the following would be the best title for the passage?
options: Does AI Enhance Scholarship or Produce Scientific “Slop”?
The Technological Revolution Behind Large Language Models
AI Tools and Their Impact on Online Search Engines
Academic Writing Standards in the Digital Age
questions.explain: Tiêu đề phù hợp nhất bao quát toàn bộ nội dung văn bản. Tác giả mở đầu bằng câu hỏi: 'Does AI improve academic writing, or does it merely produce scientific AI slop?', sau đó phân tích tính hai mặt của AI (tăng năng suất, đa dạng nguồn tài liệu, nhưng cũng che đậy bài kém chất lượng). Đáp án A phản ánh chính xác nhất chủ đề này.

questions.question: The phrase “hyperproduction” is used mainly to
options: describe improved efficiency in academic research processes
indicate higher creativity achieved through AI assistance
highlight the rapid and excessive volume of AI-generated outputs
suggest a balanced growth in scientific publishing
questions.explain: Tiền tố 'hyper-' biểu thị sự vượt mức, thái quá. 'Hyperproduction of AI slop' được dùng để nhấn mạnh khối lượng sản phẩm văn bản do AI tạo ra một cách quá nhiều, ồ ạt, nhanh chóng nhưng chất lượng thấp.

questions.question: What can be inferred from the findings about AI-written articles?
options: Journals generally prefer linguistically complex academic writing
AI helps authors improve both language quality and research depth
Articles without AI support tend to lack linguistic sophistication
Complex language generated by AI may mask weak scientific contributions
questions.explain: Đoạn 3 kết luận: 'when it comes to articles written with AI support... the more complex the language, the less likely the article was to be published. This suggests that AI-generated complex language was used to hide the low quality of the scholarly work.' (Ngôn ngữ phức tạp do AI tạo ra được dùng để che giấu những đóng góp khoa học yếu kém).

questions.question: The author most likely believes that AI in academic writing:
options: presents both opportunities for efficiency and risks to scholarly standards
should replace traditional academic writing practices entirely
mainly improves citation diversity without affecting research quality
has a uniformly positive impact across all scientific disciplines
questions.explain: Toàn bài đọc thể hiện quan điểm khách quan, hai chiều của tác giả. AI tạo ra cơ hội (opportunities) làm tăng năng suất, hỗ trợ người không dùng tiếng Anh bản xứ, đa dạng hóa nguồn; nhưng đồng thời mang tới rủi ro (risks) làm giảm tiêu chuẩn học thuật thông qua 'scientific slop'.

questions.question: Based on paragraph 4, source diversity in AI-enhanced search results ________________
options: decline because AI favors frequently cited publications
depend mainly on user-selected filtering preferences
increase due to the integration of search retrieval with generative AI
be unrelated to the use of AI-powered search tools
questions.explain: Đoạn cuối viết: 'Bing users were exposed to a greater variety of sources... This is likely caused by a technique used by Bing Chat called retrieval-augmented generation, which combines search results with AI prompting.' Nghĩa là sự đa dạng tăng lên nhờ việc tích hợp công cụ tìm kiếm với AI tạo sinh.

subject: Tiếng Anh
instruction: Questions 46-52: Read the passage carefully. Choose an option (A, B, C, or D) that best answers each question.
passage_content:
1. Fairies today are the stuff of children's stories, little magical people with wings, often shining with light. Typically pretty and female, like Tinkerbell in Peter Pan, they usually use their magic to do small things and are mostly friendly to humans.
2. We owe many of our modern ideas about fairies to Shakespeare and stories from the 18th and 19th centuries. Although we can see the origins of fairies as far back as the Ancient Greeks, we can see similar creatures in many cultures. The earliest fairy-like creatures can be found in the Greek idea that trees and rivers had spirits called dryads and nymphs. Some people think these creatures were originally the gods of earlier, pagan religions that worshipped nature. They were replaced by the Greek and Roman gods, and then later by the Christian God, and became smaller, less powerful figures as they lost importance.
3. Another explanation suggests the origin of fairies is a memory of real people, not spirits. So, for example, when tribes with metal weapons invaded land where people only used stone weapons, some of the people escaped and hid in forests and caves. Further support for this idea is that fairies were thought to be afraid of iron and could not touch it. Living outside of society, the hiding people probably stole food and attacked villages. This might explain why fairies were often described as playing tricks on humans. Hundreds of years ago, people actually believed that fairies stole new babies and replaced them with a 'changeling' - or that they took new mothers and made them feed fairy babies with their milk.
4. While most people no longer believe in fairies, only a hundred years ago some people were very willing to think they might exist. In 1917, 16-year-old Elsie Wright took two photos of her cousin, nine-year-old Frances Griffiths, sitting with fairies. Some photography experts thought they were fake, while others weren't sure. But Arthur Conan Doyle, the writer of the Sherlock Holmes detective stories, believed they were real. He published the original pictures, and three more the girls took for him, in a magazine called The Strand, in 1920. The girls only admitted the photos were fake years later in 1983, created using pictures of dancers that Elsie copied from a book.

questions.question: What is the passage mainly about?
options: The history of fairies
The Cottingley Fairies incident
The different types of fairies
The magical powers of fairies
questions.explain: Bài đọc bàn về hình ảnh các nàng tiên trong thời hiện đại (đoạn 1), nguồn gốc của chúng từ các vị thần cổ đại (đoạn 2), nguồn gốc từ ký ức về những người thật phải trốn tránh (đoạn 3), và niềm tin vào tiên nữ trong quá khứ gần đây (đoạn 4). Do đó, chủ đề chính khái quát nhất là Lịch sử của các nàng tiên (The history of fairies).

questions.question: In paragraph 2, which of the following is NOT mentioned as an early belief about fairies?
options: They were once powerful gods
They were not afraid of iron
They were associated with nature spirits
They were often depicted as small and magical
questions.explain: Trong đoạn 3, tác giả nói rõ ràng rằng: 'fairies were thought to be afraid of iron and could not touch it' (tiên nữ được cho là sợ sắt và không thể chạm vào nó). Vì vậy, nói họ không sợ sắt là thông tin KHÔNG được đề cập (hoặc sai với bài).

questions.question: What does the word "changeling" in paragraph 3 refer to?
options: A fairy disguise
A stolen human baby
A fairy baby
A magical potion
questions.explain: Đoạn 3 viết: 'fairies stole new babies and replaced them with a changeling'. Đánh cắp những em bé con người và thay thế bằng một 'changeling'. 'Changeling' ở đây ám chỉ em bé tiên (fairy baby) được để lại thay thế cho em bé loài người bị bắt đi.

questions.question: The closest meaning to the word "invaded" in paragraph 3 is:
options: attacked
visited
explored
conquered
questions.explain: Từ 'invaded' (xâm lược, tràn vào) dùng để diễn tả các bộ lạc có vũ khí kim loại tấn công tràn vào vùng đất của những người chỉ có vũ khí bằng đá. Từ 'attacked' (tấn công) là từ mang ý nghĩa gần nhất trong ngữ cảnh này.

questions.question: Which of the following is a stated detail about the beliefs of people hundreds of years ago, as mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: Fairies could control the weather
Fairies were often seen dancing in moonlight
Fairies could turn humans into animals
Fairies were believed to steal babies
questions.explain: Cuối đoạn 3 nêu rõ: 'Hundreds of years ago, people actually believed that fairies stole new babies...' (Hàng trăm năm trước, người ta thực sự tin rằng các nàng tiên hay đánh cắp trẻ sơ sinh).

questions.question: Which of the following is a stated detail about the Cottingley Fairies incident in paragraph 4?
options: The girls admitted the photos were fake in 1983
The photos were taken in a secret forest
The fairies in the photos were very large
The girls used advanced technology to create the photos
questions.explain: Câu cuối của đoạn 4 xác nhận: 'The girls only admitted the photos were fake years later in 1983' (Mãi đến năm 1983, những cô gái này mới thú nhận những bức ảnh đó là giả).

questions.question: What can be inferred about the public's belief in fairies in the early 20th century, based on paragraph 4?
options: People were skeptical about fairy lore
People were completely dismissive of fairy tales
Some people were still open to the possibility of fairies
People were more interested in scientific explanations
questions.explain: Câu mở đầu đoạn 4 viết: 'While most people no longer believe in fairies, only a hundred years ago some people were very willing to think they might exist' (Vào 100 năm trước, vẫn còn một số người rất cởi mở tin rằng tiên nữ có thể tồn tại). Arthur Conan Doyle cũng là một minh chứng.

subject: Tiếng Anh
instruction: Questions 53-60: Read the passage carefully. Choose an option (A, B, C, or D) that best answers each question.
passage_content:
1. Social media, magazines and shop windows bombard people daily with things to buy, and British consumers are buying more clothes and shoes than ever before. Online shopping means it is easy for customers to buy without thinking, while major brands offer such cheap clothes that they can be treated like disposable items worn two or three times and then thrown away.
2. In Britain, the average person spends more than £1,000 on new clothes a year, which is around four per cent of their income. That might not sound like much, but that figure hides two far more worrying trends for society and for the environment. First, a lot of that consumer spending is via credit cards. British people currently owe approximately £670 per adult to credit card companies. That's 66 per cent of the average wardrobe budget. Also, not only are people spending money they don't have, they're using it to buy things they don't need. Britain throws away 300,000 tons of clothing a year, most of which goes into landfill sites.
3. People might not realise they are part of the disposable clothing problem because they donate their unwanted clothes to charities. But charity shops can't sell all those unwanted clothes. 'Fast fashion' goes out of fashion as quickly as it came in and is often too poor quality to recycle; people don't want to buy it second-hand. Huge quantities end up being thrown away, and a lot of clothes that charities can't sell are sent abroad, causing even more economic and environmental problems.
4. However, a different trend is springing up in opposition to consumerism - the 'buy nothing' trend. The idea originated in Canada in the early 1990s and then moved to the US, where it became a rejection of the overspending and overconsumption of Black Friday and Cyber Monday during Thanksgiving weekend. On Buy Nothing Day people organise various types of protests and cut up their credit cards. Throughout the year, Buy Nothing groups organise the exchange and repair of items they already own.
5. The trend has now reached influencers on social media who usually share posts of clothing and make-up that they recommend for people to buy. Some YouTube stars now encourage their viewers not to buy anything at all for periods as long as a year. Two friends in Canada spent a year working towards buying only food. For the first three months they learned how to live without buying electrical goods, clothes or things for the house. For the next stage, they gave up services, for example haircuts, eating out at restaurants or buying petrol for their cars. In one year, they'd saved $55,000.

questions.question: The best title of the passage can be
options: "How Fast Fashion Fuels Environmental Change"
"Buy More, Spend More: Trends in British Consumerism"
"The Buy Nothing Movement"
"Fashion in Britain: New Trends and Styles"
questions.explain: Bài đọc mở đầu bằng tình trạng tiêu dùng quá mức, thời trang ăn liền (fast fashion) ở Anh gây lãng phí. Sau đó (ở đoạn 4, 5) giới thiệu về xu hướng đối lập đang lan rộng là 'The Buy Nothing Movement' (Phong trào Không Mua Sắm Gì Cả) như một giải pháp để chống lại chủ nghĩa tiêu dùng. Do đó, nhan đề bao quát nhất là C.

questions.question: The word "bombarded" in paragraph 1 can be replaced by:
options: attacked
overwhelmed
influenced
encouraged
questions.explain: Từ 'bombard' nghĩa đen là oanh tạc, ném bom. Nghĩa bóng trong câu này là mạng xã hội, tạp chí 'khủng bố' hoặc 'làm choáng ngợp' người dùng bằng hàng loạt thông tin mời gọi mua sắm. 'Overwhelmed' (áp đảo, tràn ngập) là từ đồng nghĩa sát nhất trong ngữ cảnh.

questions.question: What can be inferred from paragraph 2 about British consumer habits?
options: People are buying more clothes than they actually need
Most people are paying off their credit card debt quickly
People are saving money on clothes by shopping at charity shops
Consumers in Britain spend the most money on electronics
questions.explain: Đoạn 2 chỉ ra rằng người dân chi tiêu bằng tiền họ không có (thẻ tín dụng) để 'mua những thứ họ không cần' ('buy things they don't need'), dẫn đến vứt bỏ 300,000 tấn quần áo mỗi năm. Từ đó suy ra họ đang mua quần áo nhiều hơn so với mức thực tế họ cần.

questions.question: The phrase "fast fashion" in paragraph 3 refers to
options: Clothes that are sold at high prices
The speed at which fashion trends change
The high quality of clothes sold
Clothes that are too expensive to recycle
questions.explain: Đoạn 3 có cụm: ''Fast fashion' goes out of fashion as quickly as it came in and is often too poor quality'. Từ này ám chỉ xu hướng thời trang ăn liền - sự thay đổi quá chóng vánh của các trào lưu thời trang ('the speed at which fashion trends change').

questions.question: In paragraph 3, it is NOT mentioned that
options: Fast fashion goes out of style quickly
People throw away clothes they no longer want
Charity shops sell all the unwanted clothes they receive
Clothes donated to charity are often sent abroad
questions.explain: Trong đoạn 3, tác giả nói rõ: 'But charity shops can't sell all those unwanted clothes' (Các cửa hàng từ thiện KHÔNG THỂ bán hết những bộ quần áo thừa đó). Do vậy, câu C (Cửa hàng từ thiện bán hết quần áo họ nhận được) là thông tin sai với bài đọc.

questions.question: It can be seen in paragraphs 2, 3, and 4 that
options: People in Britain are becoming more environmentally conscious
Charity shops are a major solution to waste in the fashion industry
People are spending a significant amount of money on clothes they don't need
The Buy Nothing trend is causing people to stop shopping altogether
questions.explain: Thông tin xuyên suốt các đoạn là việc người dân lãng phí tiền bạc vào quần áo nhanh, bỏ đi một số lượng rác thải khổng lồ. Mọi người tiêu hàng ngàn bảng Anh (khoảng tiền đáng kể) cho những thứ họ không thực sự cần ('buy things they don't need').

questions.question: In paragraph 5, the author uses the word "encourage" in order to
options: Explain how people can save money
Show how influencers promote consumerism
Discuss the financial success of YouTube stars
Highlight how some influencers support the Buy Nothing trend
questions.explain: Từ 'encourage' (khuyến khích) xuất hiện trong câu: 'Some YouTube stars now encourage their viewers not to buy anything at all...'. Ý của tác giả là để nhấn mạnh việc ngay cả những người có sức ảnh hưởng trên mạng xã hội (vốn thường quảng cáo bán hàng) giờ đây cũng quay sang ủng hộ phong trào Không Mua Sắm (Buy Nothing).

questions.question: It can be inferred from paragraph 5 that
options: The Buy Nothing trend is not popular in Canada
Influencers are encouraging people to spend more money on their clothes
People in Britain and Canada are abandoning the Buy Nothing trend after a year
Some people are willing to sacrifice material goods for financial gain
questions.explain: Đoạn 5 đưa ra ví dụ về hai người bạn ở Canada quyết định hy sinh nhiều thứ vật chất (đồ điện tử, quần áo, làm tóc, ăn hàng, đổ xăng) trong một năm và kết quả là 'In one year, they'd saved $55,000' (họ tiết kiệm được 55.000 đô la). Từ đó suy ra một số người sẵn sàng từ bỏ lợi ích vật chất để đổi lấy lợi ích tài chính lớn hơn.

subject: Tiếng Anh
instruction: Questions 16-22: Read the passage carefully. Choose an option (A, B, C, or D) that best answers each question.
passage_content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is rapidly reshaping the modern workplace, bringing both unprecedented opportunities and significant challenges. By automating routine and repetitive tasks, AI allows employees to focus on more complex, creative, and strategic activities. For instance, in customer service, AI-powered chatbots can handle basic inquiries, freeing human agents to deal with more nuanced customer issues. This shift not only increases productivity but also has the potential to make work more engaging for individuals.
However, the integration of AI also raises concerns about job displacement. Many workers fear that as machines become smarter, human roles will become obsolete. While it is true that certain jobs, particularly those involving manual data entry or basic assembly line work, are at risk, historical trends suggest a different outcome. Every major technological revolution, from the steam engine to the internet, has ultimately created more jobs than it destroyed. AI is expected to follow this pattern by generating new roles such as data ethicists, AI trainers, and machine learning engineers.
The key to surviving and thriving in this new era lies in adaptability and continuous learning. Workers must be willing to upskill and reskill to remain relevant. Educational institutions and corporations alike share the responsibility of providing training programs that equip the workforce with the necessary digital literacy and uniquely human skills, like emotional intelligence and critical thinking, which machines cannot replicate. Ultimately, the future of work is not about humans competing with machines, but rather humans collaborating with them to achieve greater efficiency and innovation.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: AI will eventually replace all human jobs in the customer service sector.
AI is transforming the workplace by automating tasks and requiring humans to adapt.
The primary goal of AI is to eliminate repetitive tasks and reduce company costs.
Educational institutions are failing to prepare workers for the AI revolution.
questions.explain: Bài đọc thảo luận về cách AI đang định hình lại nơi làm việc (tự động hóa các nhiệm vụ lặp đi lặp lại, tạo ra cơ hội mới) và nhấn mạnh rằng con người cần phải thích nghi (upskill, reskill) để hợp tác với AI. Đáp án B bao quát đầy đủ nhất các ý này.

questions.question: According to paragraph 1, what is a benefit of using AI-powered chatbots in customer service?
options: They completely replace human agents.
They make work more repetitive for human agents.
They handle basic inquiries, allowing humans to tackle complex issues.
They reduce the need for creative and strategic activities.
questions.explain: Đoạn 1 viết: 'AI-powered chatbots can handle basic inquiries, freeing human agents to deal with more nuanced customer issues'. (Chatbot xử lý các câu hỏi cơ bản, giải phóng con người để giải quyết các vấn đề phức tạp hơn).

questions.question: The word "obsolete" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: updated
outdated
important
demanding
questions.explain: Từ 'obsolete' có nghĩa là lỗi thời, không còn được sử dụng nữa. Trong ngữ cảnh này, công nhân lo sợ vai trò của con người sẽ trở nên 'outdated' (lỗi thời) khi máy móc thông minh hơn.

questions.question: The word "them" in the last sentence refers to:
options: human skills
training programs
educational institutions
machines
questions.explain: Câu cuối: '...humans collaborating with them to achieve greater efficiency...'. 'Them' ở đây thay thế cho 'machines' (máy móc) đã được nhắc đến ở vế trước.

questions.question: According to the passage, what does historical trend suggest about technological revolutions?
options: They always lead to long-term massive unemployment.
They destroy more jobs than they create.
They only benefit highly educated workers.
They eventually create more jobs than they eliminate.
questions.explain: Đoạn 2 nêu rõ: 'Every major technological revolution... has ultimately created more jobs than it destroyed' (Mọi cuộc cách mạng công nghệ lớn cuối cùng đều tạo ra nhiều việc làm hơn là phá hủy chúng).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a uniquely human skill?
options: Emotional intelligence
Critical thinking
Manual data entry
Adaptability
questions.explain: Đoạn 3 liệt kê các kỹ năng đặc thù của con người (uniquely human skills) như 'emotional intelligence' và 'critical thinking'. Khả năng thích nghi (adaptability) cũng được nhấn mạnh. Trong khi đó, 'manual data entry' (nhập liệu thủ công) ở đoạn 2 được nhắc đến như một công việc có nguy cơ bị máy móc thay thế.

questions.question: What is the author's attitude towards the integration of AI in the workplace?
options: Highly pessimistic
Cautiously optimistic
Indifferent
Fearful
questions.explain: Tác giả thừa nhận những thách thức (job displacement) nhưng lập luận rằng theo lịch sử, công nghệ sẽ tạo ra việc làm mới và con người có thể phát triển nếu biết thích nghi, học hỏi. Thái độ này mang tính lạc quan thận trọng (Cautiously optimistic).

subject: Tiếng Anh
instruction: Questions 23-30: Read the passage carefully. Choose an option (A, B, C, or D) that best answers each question.
passage_content:
Sleep is a fundamental human need, yet it is often the first thing we sacrifice in our busy, modern lives. While many view sleep merely as a period of rest, scientists understand it as an incredibly active state for both the brain and the body. A normal sleep cycle consists of two main types of sleep: Non-Rapid Eye Movement (NREM) and Rapid Eye Movement (REM) sleep. During the night, we cycle through these stages multiple times, and each plays a critical role in our overall health.
NREM sleep, which makes up about 75% of our sleep time, is primarily responsible for physical restoration. During its deepest stages, the body repairs tissues, builds bone and muscle, and strengthens the immune system. REM sleep, on the other hand, is crucial for cognitive functions. It is during REM sleep that the brain consolidates memories, processes emotions, and clears out neurological toxins accumulated during waking hours. Without sufficient REM sleep, individuals often struggle with memory retention and emotional regulation.
Chronic sleep deprivation has profound consequences on mental and physical well-being. Short-term effects include irritability, lack of focus, and impaired judgment. Over the long term, insufficient sleep is linked to severe health issues, including obesity, cardiovascular disease, and severe depression. Furthermore, chronic lack of sleep elevates cortisol levels, a stress hormone, which keeps the body in a constant state of high alert and anxiety.
Health experts generally recommend that adults aim for seven to nine hours of quality sleep per night. To achieve this, maintaining good 'sleep hygiene' is essential. This includes establishing a consistent sleep schedule, keeping the bedroom dark and cool, and avoiding blue light emitted by screens for at least an hour before bedtime. By prioritizing sleep, individuals can significantly enhance their quality of life, productivity, and long-term health.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the passage?
options: To explain the causes of sleep disorders.
To compare NREM and REM sleep patterns.
To highlight the importance of sleep and the dangers of sleep deprivation.
To provide a medical guide for curing insomnia.
questions.explain: Bài đọc giải thích vai trò của các chu kỳ giấc ngủ (NREM và REM) đối với sức khỏe, hậu quả nghiêm trọng của việc thiếu ngủ, và cách cải thiện chất lượng giấc ngủ. Mục đích chính là nhấn mạnh tầm quan trọng của giấc ngủ và sự nguy hiểm của việc thiếu ngủ.

questions.question: According to paragraph 2, what is a primary function of NREM sleep?
options: Processing complex emotions
Consolidating daily memories
Clearing out neurological toxins
Repairing tissues and strengthening the immune system
questions.explain: Đoạn 2 nêu rõ: 'NREM sleep... is primarily responsible for physical restoration. During its deepest stages, the body repairs tissues, builds bone and muscle, and strengthens the immune system' (Giấc ngủ NREM chịu trách nhiệm phục hồi thể chất, sửa chữa mô, tăng cường hệ miễn dịch).

questions.question: The word "consolidates" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: strengthens and secures
erases and forgets
creates and invents
separates and divides
questions.explain: Từ 'consolidates' có nghĩa là củng cố, làm cho vững chắc. Trong ngữ cảnh 'the brain consolidates memories', nó có nghĩa là não bộ củng cố và lưu giữ lại các ký ức một cách chắc chắn (strengthens and secures).

questions.question: What happens if a person does not get enough REM sleep?
options: Their muscles fail to grow.
They may have trouble remembering things and controlling emotions.
Their immune system immediately shuts down.
They will sleepwalk during the night.
questions.explain: Đoạn 2 nêu: 'Without sufficient REM sleep, individuals often struggle with memory retention and emotional regulation' (Nếu thiếu giấc ngủ REM, con người sẽ gặp khó khăn trong việc ghi nhớ và điều chỉnh cảm xúc).

questions.question: The word "deprivation" in paragraph 3 most likely means:
options: abundance
lack or shortage
overconsumption
improvement
questions.explain: Từ 'deprivation' nghĩa là sự thiếu hụt, tước đoạt. 'Sleep deprivation' là tình trạng thiếu ngủ.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as a long-term consequence of poor sleep?
options: Obesity
Cardiovascular disease
Vision loss
Severe depression
questions.explain: Đoạn 3 liệt kê các hậu quả lâu dài bao gồm: obesity (béo phì), cardiovascular disease (bệnh tim mạch), và severe depression (trầm cảm nặng). Việc giảm thị lực (Vision loss) không được nhắc đến.

questions.question: According to the passage, why is it advised to avoid screens before bedtime?
options: They make the bedroom too warm.
They emit blue light which negatively affects sleep hygiene.
They cause immediate long-term depression.
They increase the amount of NREM sleep excessively.
questions.explain: Đoạn cuối khuyên: 'avoiding blue light emitted by screens for at least an hour before bedtime' để duy trì 'sleep hygiene' (thói quen ngủ tốt). Ánh sáng xanh từ màn hình gây ảnh hưởng tiêu cực đến chất lượng giấc ngủ.

questions.question: What can be inferred from the passage about the relationship between cortisol and sleep?
options: High cortisol levels help individuals sleep more deeply.
Cortisol is the hormone responsible for repairing tissues during NREM sleep.
A chronic lack of sleep leads to higher stress levels due to elevated cortisol.
Cortisol clears out neurological toxins from the brain.
questions.explain: Đoạn 3 nêu: 'chronic lack of sleep elevates cortisol levels, a stress hormone, which keeps the body in a constant state of high alert and anxiety' (Thiếu ngủ mãn tính làm tăng nồng độ cortisol, một loại hormone căng thẳng, giữ cơ thể ở trạng thái cảnh giác cao và lo âu). Điều này suy ra thiếu ngủ dẫn đến mức độ căng thẳng cao hơn do cortisol.
